label: eliza-1-27b
notes:
2x H200 SXM at FP8 + FP8 KV. The fp8 sibling repo is produced by `scripts/publish_all_eliza1.sh --filter-size 27b --filter-quant fp8`.

--calculate-kv-scales omitted per vllm#37554: hybrid Gated-DeltaNet + Gated-Attention models corrupt the GDN recurrent state during FP8 scale calibration (uninitialized `conv_state`/`ssm_state` in the dummy pass produces garbage scales). Default scales (1.0) keep bf16 KV activations within FP8 E4M3's ±448 range. Re-enable only after a regression run that calibrates GDN layers separately.

KV dtype stays fp8_e4m3 rather than turboquant_4bit_nc (PR #38479 merged in v0.20.1) because per omlx#825 hybrid GDN+attention + prefix caching loses tool-calling on cache hits, and vLLM PR #40898 (DFlash + SWA) is still open at time of writing. Reassess after that lands.

GGUF / llama-server path on a single RTX 5090 (the existing eliza/cloud/services/vast-pyworker/onstart.sh template): set MODEL_REPO=elizalabs/eliza-1-27b-gguf-q6_k, MODEL_FILE=eliza-1-27b-Q6_K.gguf, image ghcr.io/ggml-org/llama.cpp:server-cuda. That path matches the upstream pyworker contract directly.

WORKER CONTRACT: vLLM on port 8000. Deploy with the sibling `onstart-vllm.sh` (declared via `onstart_script`); it builds the vllm argv from this manifest's `vllm_args`, tails for `Application startup complete`, and execs the upstream pyworker (`worker.py`) with LLAMA_SERVER_PORT=$VLLM_PORT so the heartbeat contract still works.
